cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage again to find the correct answer. Germany - Parents can't use last names and the names of objects and products as first names. A child's first name must clearly indicate his or her sex, and all names must be approved by the office of vital statistics in the area in which the child was born. Iceland - The country's naming committee consults the National Register of Persons to determine if a name is acceptable. If parents want to go "off-list," they must apply for approval and pay a fee, and the name must contain only letters in the Icelandic alphabet. New Zealand -
